planting_details.assumptions_or_notes: Assumed rainfed smallholder system in Kitale highlands using Kenya Seed H614; main season is long rains (Mar–Jun). Chosen PPOP (5.0 plants/m²) with 75 cm rows implies ≈27 cm in-row spacing, within common 25–30 cm practice. Planting depth 5 cm reflects hand-hoeing in moist soils. Row direction unknown; set to −99. Actual 2010 rainfall onset may vary by field; if using site-specific rainfall, adjust PDATE accordingly.

events.Assumptions_or_Notes: Basal DAP (diammonium phosphate, 18-46-0) placed in the planting hole just below/offset from the seed (hand placement). Product-rate logic: ~50 kg/ha DAP supplies 9.0 kg N/ha and ≈10.0 kg P/ha (P2O5 fraction 0.46 × 0.4364). This reflects common Kitale farmer practice of DAP at planting for P. Depth set to ~5 cm to reflect fertilizer in the bottom of the hole.

Topdress CAN (calcium ammonium nitrate, 26-0-0) at ~35 DAP (knee-high). Broadcast by hand and lightly incorporated during weeding. Product-rate equivalence: ≈177 kg/ha CAN to supply 46.0 kg N/ha (0.26 N fraction). This timing and method are typical in Trans-Nzoia highlands; K is not routinely applied. Total N across events equals exactly 55.0 kg N/ha per experimental requirement.
